section: Plan and manage an Azure AI solution
stem: In the code shown, which value is being used as the credential for the OpenAI client?
code:
from openai import OpenAI
from azure.identity import DefaultAzureCredential, get_bearer_token_provider

token_provider = get_bearer_token_provider(
    DefaultAzureCredential(),
    "https://ai.azure.com/.default"
)

client = OpenAI(
    base_url="https://contoso.openai.azure.com/openai/v1/",
    api_key=token_provider,
)
options: A Microsoft Entra token provider created from DefaultAzureCredential
An Azure AI Search query key
explanation: The get_bearer_token_provider call wraps DefaultAzureCredential and supplies Microsoft Entra tokens for keyless authentication.
source: Original AI-103 practice item aligned to Microsoft Learn skills measured

stem: Which reasoning effort value is configured in this request?
code:
response = client.chat.completions.create(
    model="gpt-5-mini",
    messages=[
        {"role": "developer", "content": "You are a helpful assistant."},
        {"role": "user", "content": "Create an implementation checklist."},
    ],
    max_completion_tokens=5000,
    reasoning_effort="medium",
)
options: The code does not configure reasoning effort
explanation: The request explicitly sets reasoning_effort to medium.

section: Plan and manage an Azure AI solution
stem: What security weakness should you identify in this code for a production app?
code:
from azure.core.credentials import AzureKeyCredential
from azure.search.documents import SearchClient

search_client = SearchClient(
    endpoint="https://contoso.search.windows.net",
    index_name="policies",
    credential=AzureKeyCredential("1234567890abcdef")
)
options: It embeds a query key directly in the application code
It uses too many selected fields
explanation: Keys should not be hardcoded in production code. Prefer managed identity/RBAC where supported or secure secret storage.
source: Original AI-103 practice item aligned to Microsoft Learn skills measured

stem: What is the maximum number of documents this query asks Azure AI Search to return?
code:
results = search_client.search(
    search_text="historic hotel walk to restaurants and shopping",
    vector_queries=[vector_query],
    select=["HotelId", "HotelName", "Description", "Category", "Tags"],
    top=5,
    include_total_count=True
)
explanation: The top parameter limits the returned results to five documents.

section: Implement generative AI and agentic solutions
stem: What makes this Azure AI Search query a hybrid query?
code:
from azure.search.documents.models import VectorizedQuery

vector_query = VectorizedQuery(
    vector=query_vector,
    k_nearest_neighbors=10,
    fields="DescriptionVector",
    exhaustive=True
)

results = client.search(
    search_text="historic hotel walk to restaurants and shopping",
    vector_queries=[vector_query],
    select=["HotelName", "Description", "Address/City"],
    top=10
)
options: It sends both search_text and vector_queries
explanation: Hybrid search combines keyword/full-text input with vector retrieval in the same request.
source: Original AI-103 practice item aligned to Microsoft Learn skills measured

section: Implement generative AI and agentic solutions
stem: What is the purpose of WebSearchTool() in this Foundry agent definition?
code:
from azure.identity import DefaultAzureCredential
from azure.ai.projects import AIProjectClient
from azure.ai.projects.models import PromptAgentDefinition, WebSearchTool

project = AIProjectClient(
    endpoint=PROJECT_ENDPOINT,
    credential=DefaultAzureCredential(),
)

agent = project.agents.create_version(
    agent_name="web-search-agent",
    definition=PromptAgentDefinition(
        model="gpt-4.1-mini",
        instructions="You are a helpful assistant that can search the web.",
        tools=[WebSearchTool()],
    ),
)
options: It enables the agent to retrieve public web information through a configured tool
explanation: Tools extend agent capabilities beyond the base model.
source: Original AI-103 practice item aligned to Microsoft Learn skills measured

section: Implement generative AI and agentic solutions
stem: Which parameter enables semantic ranking in this query?
code:
results = search_client.search(
    search_text="historic hotel walk to restaurants and shopping",
    vector_queries=[vector_query],
    select=["HotelId", "HotelName", "Category", "Description"],
    query_type="semantic",
    semantic_configuration_name="my-semantic-config",
    top=5,
    include_total_count=True
)
explanation: Semantic ranking is requested by setting query_type to semantic with a semantic configuration.
source: Original AI-103 practice item aligned to Microsoft Learn skills measured

section: Implement generative AI and agentic solutions
stem: When is the geo.distance filter applied in this query?
code:
results = search_client.search(
    include_total_count=True,
    top=5,
    select=["HotelId", "HotelName", "Description"],
    filter="geo.distance(Location, geography'POINT(-77.03241 38.90166)') le 300",
    vector_filter_mode="postFilter",
    vector_queries=[vector_query]
)
options: After vector search, because vector_filter_mode is postFilter

explanation: STT converts audio input to text.
source: Original AI-103 practice item aligned to Microsoft Learn skills measured

section: Implement text analysis solutions
stem: What does this code primarily perform?
code:
import os
import azure.cognitiveservices.speech as speechsdk

speech_config = speechsdk.SpeechConfig(
    subscription=os.environ.get("SPEECH_KEY"),
    endpoint=os.environ.get("ENDPOINT")
)
audio_config = speechsdk.audio.AudioOutputConfig(use_default_speaker=True)
speech_config.speech_synthesis_voice_name = "en-US-AvaNeural"

synthesizer = speechsdk.SpeechSynthesizer(
    speech_config=speech_config,
    audio_config=audio_config
)
result = synthesizer.speak_text_async("Your order has shipped.").get()
explanation: SpeechSynthesizer.speak_text_async synthesizes audio from text.
source: Original AI-103 practice item aligned to Microsoft Learn skills measured

stem: Which two field names are requested by query_fields?
code:
poller = document_intelligence_client.begin_analyze_document(
    "prebuilt-layout",
    AnalyzeDocumentRequest(url_source=formUrl),
    features=[DocumentAnalysisFeature.QUERY_FIELDS],
    query_fields=["Address", "InvoiceNumber"],
)
explanation: The query_fields list contains Address and InvoiceNumber.

section: Implement information extraction solutions
stem: Which Content Understanding analyzer is selected?
code:
poller = client.begin_analyze(
    analyzer_id="prebuilt-invoice",
    inputs=[AnalysisInput(url=invoice_url)],
)
result = poller.result()
explanation: The begin_analyze call sets analyzer_id to prebuilt-invoice.
source: Original AI-103 practice item aligned to Microsoft Learn skills measured

section: Implement information extraction solutions
stem: Which property is printed as markdown content from the Content Understanding result?
code:
poller = await client.begin_analyze(
    analyzer_id="prebuilt-documentSearch",
    inputs=[AnalysisInput(url=file_url)]
)
result = await poller.result()
content = result.contents[0]
print(content.markdown)
explanation: The documentSearch example prints content.markdown for downstream document representation.
source: Original AI-103 practice item aligned to Microsoft Learn skills measured
